Revenue.title: Revenue
Revenue.description: Represents a single revenue stream for the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the revenue entry.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents a single expense incurred by the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expense entry.

category.description: Category of the expense (e.g., supplies, rent, utilities).

startTime.description: The starting date and time of the appointment.

endTime.description: The ending date and time of the appointment.

attendees.description: Attendees of the appointment. Can be a list of names or references to a User/Contact entity.

definition.description: Collection to store user profiles. (Although the user entity wasn't explicitly defined, this path is necessary for user-specific data.)

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to support a basic ERP system for small businesses, focusing on revenue, expenses, and appointments.  User data is segregated under `/users/{userId}` for private data. Revenue, Expenses, and Appointments are stored as subcollections under each user's document to ensure data isolation and ownership. This structure facilitates simple security rules based on the authenticated user's ID. The structure enforces Authorization Independence. No `get()` operations are required in rules. Each collection under `/users/{userId}` is effectively private to that user, enabling straightforward read/write rules based on `request.auth.uid`. QAPs are supported because list operations will be scoped to the user's own data, ensuring that users can only access their own revenue, expenses, and appointments.
